body:
{
  "title": "Bex Leclerc — Founder & CEO, Forge Build",
  "compiled_truth": "Bex Leclerc is the founder and CEO of [Forge Build](companies/forge-build), a developer tools company she started in 2024 after stints at various infrastructure and developer-tools shops. She's known across the ecosystem as a storyteller first, engineer second — though people who've worked with her say that undersells her technical instincts. Bex has a gift for distilling complex infrastructure problems into narratives that resonate with developers, which is partly why Forge Build's early traction was so organic.

Before founding Forge Build, Leclerc bounced between a handful of infra companies, never staying more than two years at any of them. She's talked openly about how those experiences shaped her conviction that developer tooling was fundamentally misaligned with how engineers actually build. "Every tool I used felt like it was designed for a demo, not a Tuesday at 2am when prod is on fire," she said at a 2027 conference. That philosophy — tooling for the worst moments, not the best ones — runs through everything Forge Build ships.

Her relationship with the [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) team goes back to mid-2026, when early conversations about integration surfaced mutual interests. Forge Build's developer workflow tooling and Anchor's deterministic data infrastructure turned out to be surprisingly complementary. Bex was one of the first external founders to get access to Anchor's single-pass primitives in a sandbox environment, and she's been vocal about what that unlocked for Forge Build's pipeline reliability.

Personality-wise, Leclerc is product-obsessed to a degree that sometimes frustrates her own team. She's known to rewrite onboarding flows herself at odd hours, and she insists on reading every piece of user feedback that comes through support channels. Her storytelling ability extends to fundraising — she closed Forge Build's seed round in under three weeks, reportedly without a traditional deck. Just a live demo and a conversation.

She splits time between two cities and is notoriously difficult to pin down for meetings that aren't about product. People who've partnered with her describe her as intense but deeply loyal once trust is established. She's become a connector figure in the broader infra-tools community, frequently introducing founders to each other and hosting small dinners for people building in adjacent spaces.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-05-14** | Bex Leclerc first connects with [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) team at an infra-focused founder dinner in the northeast.
- **2026-08-02** | [Forge Build](companies/forge-build) gets early sandbox access to Anchor's single-pass primitives for internal testing.
- **2026-11-19** | Bex gives a talk titled "Tooling for the Worst Day" at a developer infrastructure summit; draws significant attention from enterprise buyers.
- **2027-01-30** | Forge Build closes a funding round; Leclerc reportedly ran the process in under three weeks with no formal deck.
- **2027-04-11** | Hosts a small dinner for ~12 infra founders; attendees include several [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) team members.
- **2027-07-22** | Forge Build ships a major pipeline reliability feature built on top of deterministic data primitives; Bex announces it via a long-form blog post that goes semi-viral.
- **2027-10-05** | Leclerc joins a panel on developer experience alongside other founder-CEOs at a private industry event.
- **2028-02-18** | [Forge Build](companies/forge-build) crosses a notable enterprise adoption milestone; Bex credits tight integration partnerships as a key factor.
- **2028-06-09** | Bex keynotes a developer tools conference, focusing on how deterministic infrastructure changed her company's architectural assumptions."
}
